devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वैदर्भ्याः स तु सन्देशं निशम्य यदुनन्दनः । प्रगृह्य पाणिना पाणिं प्रहसन्निदमब्रवीत् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vaidarbhyāḥ sa tu sandeśaṃ niśamya yadunandanaḥ pragṛhya pāṇinā pāṇiṃ prahasannidamabravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the message of Rukmiṇī, the princess of Vidarbha, Kṛṣṇa, the delight of Yadus, clasped the hand of the Brāhmaṇa and laughing heartily, he spoke to him.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:]
1(A). Just as the most beautiful Rukmiṇī has ever fixed her heart on Me.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । तथाहमपि तच्चित्तो निद्रां च न लभे निशि । वेदाहम्रुक्मिणा द्वेषान्ममोद्वाहो निवारितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca tathāhamapi taccitto nidrāṃ ca na labhe niśi vedāhamrukmiṇā dveṣānmamodvāho nivāritaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
So also I too have set My heart on her. Hence I do not get any sleep at night. I know that it is due to Rukmin’s hatred against me that my marriage with her is obstructed by him.

devanagari: तामानयिष्य उन्मथ्य राजन्यापसदान्मृधे । मत्परामनवद्याङ्गी मेधसोऽग्निशिखामिव ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmānayiṣya unmathya rājanyāpasadānmṛdhe matparāmanavadyāṅgī medhaso
translation: As one would capture a flame after churning the fuel, I will vanquish those wretched kings in a battle and take away (as prize) that faultlessly beautiful princess who is so devoted to me.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । उद्वाहर्क्षं च विज्ञाय रुक्मिण्या मधुसूदनः । रथः संयुज्यतामाशु दारुकेत्याह सारथिम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca udvāharkṣaṃ ca vijñāya rukmiṇyā madhusūdanaḥ rathaḥ saṃyujyatāmāśu dāruketyāha sārathim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
Having ascertained (from the Brāhmaṇa) the constellation under which RukmiṇīṆ marriage was to take place (viz. day after tomorrow at night. Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of the demon Madhu ordered his charioteer Dāruka to get his chariot ready forthwith.

devanagari: स चाश्वैः शैब्यसुग्रीव मेघपुष्पबलाहकैः । युक्तं रथमुपानीय तस्थौ प्राञ्जलिरग्रतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cāśvaiḥ śaibyasugrīva meghapuṣpabalāhakaiḥ yuktaṃ rathamupānīya tasthau prāñjaliragrataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Dāruka instantly brought up the chariot drawn by (the famous horses) Śaibya, Sugrīva, Meghapuṣpa and Balāhaka and stood before him with folded palms.

devanagari: आरुह्य स्यन्दनं शौरिर्द्विजमारोप्य तूर्णगैः । आनर्तादेकरात्रेण विदर्भानगमद्धयैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: āruhya syandanaṃ śaurirdvijamāropya tūrṇagaiḥ ānartādekarātreṇa vidarbhānagamaddhayaiḥ
translation: Taking up the Brāhmaṇa with him, Kṛṣṇa, mounted the chariot and with those swift horses (yoked to the chariot) he travelled from Ānarta[1] to Vidarbha within one night.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Gujarat and a part of Malwa with Kuśasthalī or Dvārakā as the capital—GDAMI p. 7.

devanagari: राजा स कुण्डिनपतिः पुत्रस्नेहवशानुगः । शिशुपालाय स्वां कन्यां दास्यन्कर्माण्यकारयत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājā sa kuṇḍinapatiḥ putrasnehavaśānugaḥ śiśupālāya svāṃ kanyāṃ dāsyankarmāṇyakārayat
translation: Due to his weakness of being over-affectionate to his son, the king of Kuṇḍina caused all the auspicious rites connected with marriage-ceremony, with a view to give his daughter to Śiśupāla.

devanagari: पुरं सम्मृष्टसंसिक्त मार्गरथ्याचतुष्पथम् । चित्रध्वजपताकाभिस्तोरणैः समलङ्कृतम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: puraṃ sammṛṣṭasaṃsikta mārgarathyācatuṣpatham citradhvajapatākābhistoraṇaiḥ samalaṅkṛtam
translation: The city had all its streets, roads and quadrangles profusely sprinkled with water. It was decorated with banners of various kinds and buntings and arches. It was crowded by men and women who were wearing garlands of flowers, fragrant ointments and dressed themselves with immaculate clothes. They perfumed their mansions with the fumes of aloe wood (Aguru) smoke.

devanagari: स्रग्गन्धमाल्याभरणैर्विरजोऽम्बरभूषितैः । जुष्टं स्त्रीपुरुषैः श्रीमद् गृहैरगुरुधूपितैः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sraggandhamālyābharaṇairvirajo juṣṭaṃ strīpuruṣaiḥ śrīmad gṛhairagurudhūpitaiḥ

translation: The city had all its streets, roads and quadrangles profusely sprinkled with water. It was decorated with banners of various kinds and buntings and arches. It was crowded by men and women who were wearing garlands of flowers, fragrant ointments and dressed themselves with immaculate clothes. They perfumed their mansions with the fumes of aloe wood (Aguru) smoke.

devanagari: पितॄन्देवान्समभ्यर्च्य विप्रांश्च विधिवन्नृप । भोजयित्वा यथान्यायं वाचयामास मङ्गलम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: pitṝndevānsamabhyarcya viprāṃśca vidhivannṛpa bhojayitvā yathānyāyaṃ vācayāmāsa maṅgalam
translation: Having worshipped the manes, gods and Brāhmaṇas with due formalities and giving them a heavy feast he caused the auspicious verses recited according to formalities (to bless the bride).

devanagari: सुस्नातां सुदतीं कन्यां कृतकौतुकमङ्गलाम् । आहतांशुकयुग्मेन भूषितां भूषणोत्तमैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: susnātāṃ sudatīṃ kanyāṃ kṛtakautukamaṅgalām āhatāṃśukayugmena bhūṣitāṃ bhūṣaṇottamaiḥ
translation: The bride of beautiful teeth who had taken a bath and had gone through the formal auspicious preliminaries (such as wearing an auspicious thread woven with a piece of gold) was clothed in a pair of brand-new silken pieces and was beautified with excellent ornaments.

devanagari: चक्रुः सामर्ग्यजुर्मन्त्रैर्वध्वा रक्षां द्विजोत्तमाः । पुरोहितोऽथर्वविद्वै जुहाव ग्रहशान्तये ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: cakruḥ sāmargyajurmantrairvadhvā rakṣāṃ dvijottamāḥ purohito

translation: For the protection of the bride (from evil influences), the most prominent Brāhmaṇas pronounced mantras from the Sāmaveda, the Ṛg-Veda and the Yajur-Veda. The family-priest of the king who was expert in the Atharva-Veda offered oblations to fire for the appeasement of evil stars (and the prosperity of the house).

devanagari: हिरण्यरूप्य वासांसि तिलांश्च गुडमिश्रितान् । प्रादाद्धेनूश्च विप्रेभ्यो राजा विधिविदां वरः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyarūpya vāsāṃsi tilāṃśca guḍamiśritān prādāddhenūśca viprebhyo rājā vidhividāṃ varaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: The king who was prominent among those who were well versed in Śāstric prescriptions gave as gift to Brāhmaṇas gold, silver, clothes and sesamum seeds mixed with raw sugar and cows as well.

devanagari: एवं चेदिपती राजा दमघोषः सुताय वै । कारयामास मन्त्रज्ञैः सर्वमभ्युदयोचितम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ cedipatī rājā damaghoṣaḥ sutāya vai kārayāmāsa mantrajñaiḥ sarvamabhyudayocitam

translation: In the same way king Damaghoṣa, the ruler of Cedis, caused all the religious formalities conducive to the good of his son, by Brāhmaṇas who were expert in mantras.

devanagari: मदच्युद्भिर्गजानीकैः स्यन्दनैर्हेममालिभिः । पत्त्यश्वसङ्कुलैः सैन्यैः परीतः कुण्डिनं ययौ ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: madacyudbhirgajānīkaiḥ syandanairhemamālibhiḥ pattyaśvasaṅkulaiḥ sainyaiḥ parītaḥ kuṇḍinaṃ yayau

translation: Accompanied with regiments of elephants exuding ichor, chariots decorated with garlands of gold and with armies of cavalry and infantry, he came to the city of Kuṇḍina.

devanagari: तं वै विदर्भाधिपतिः समभ्येत्याभिपूज्य च । निवेशयामास मुदा कल्पितान्यनिवेशने ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vai vidarbhādhipatiḥ samabhyetyābhipūjya ca niveśayāmāsa mudā kalpitānyaniveśane

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ kḷp ], [vocative plural from √ kḷp ], [accusative plural from √ kḷp ]

translation: The king of Vidarbhas went out to receive him and worshipped him with all the formalities with a gladsome heart. He lodged him in a separated mansion which was reserved for that purpose.

devanagari: तत्र शाल्वो जरासन्धो दन्तवक्त्रो विदूरथः । आजग्मुश्चैद्यपक्षीयाः पौण्ड्रकाद्याः सहस्रशः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra śālvo jarāsandho dantavaktro vidūrathaḥ ājagmuścaidyapakṣīyāḥ pauṇḍrakādyāḥ sahasraśaḥ
translation:
There came thousands of kings who were the allies of Śiśupāla such as Śālva, Jarāsandha, Dantavaktra, Vidūratha, Pauṇḍraka and others.
18-19 All those enemies of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma determined to see to it that the bride was secured for Śiśupāla. They decided among themselves, “In case Kṛṣṇa accompanied by Balarāma and other Yadus comes and tries to carry away the bride, we shall jointly put up a fight with him.” Having made up their minds thus, all the kings came with all their forces and vehicles there.

devanagari: कृष्णरामद्विषो यत्ताः कन्यां चैद्याय साधितुम् । यद्यागत्य हरेत्कृष्णो रामाद्यैर्यदुभिर्वृतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇarāmadviṣo yattāḥ kanyāṃ caidyāya sādhitum yadyāgatya haretkṛṣṇo rāmādyairyadubhirvṛtaḥ

devanagari: योत्स्यामः संहतास्तेन इति निश्चितमानसाः । आजग्मुर्भूभुजः सर्वे समग्रबलवाहनाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yotsyāmaḥ saṃhatāstena iti niścitamānasāḥ ājagmurbhūbhujaḥ sarve samagrabalavāhanāḥ

devanagari: श्रुत्वैतद्भगवान्रामो विपक्षीय नृपोद्यमम् । कृष्णं चैकं गतं हर्तुं कन्यां कलहशङ्कितः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvaitadbhagavānrāmo vipakṣīya nṛpodyamam kṛṣṇaṃ caikaṃ gataṃ hartuṃ kanyāṃ kalahaśaṅkitaḥ
translation: Hearing the report of military preparations of the hostile kings, the glorious Balarāma who came to know of Kṛṣṇa’s having gone alone to carry away the bride, apprehended a fight, and taking with him a very big army consisting of elephants, horsemen, chariots and foot-soldiers quickly rushed to Kuṇḍina, as he was overcome with brotherly affection.

devanagari: बलेन महता सार्धं भ्रातृस्नेहपरिप्लुतः । त्वरितः कुण्डिनं प्रागाद्गजाश्वरथपत्तिभिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: balena mahatā sārdhaṃ bhrātṛsnehapariplutaḥ tvaritaḥ kuṇḍinaṃ prāgādgajāśvarathapattibhiḥ

translation: Hearing the report of military preparations of the hostile kings, the glorious Balarāma who came to know of Kṛṣṇa’s having gone alone to carry away the bride, apprehended a fight, and taking with him a very big army consisting of elephants, horsemen, chariots and foot-soldiers quickly rushed to Kuṇḍina, as he was overcome with brotherly affection.

devanagari: भीष्मकन्या वरारोहा काङ्क्षन्त्यागमनं हरेः । प्रत्यापत्तिमपश्यन्ती द्विजस्याचिन्तयत्तदा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīṣmakanyā varārohā kāṅkṣantyāgamanaṃ hareḥ pratyāpattimapaśyantī dvijasyācintayattadā

translation: The beautiful daughter of king Bhīṣmaka who was yearning for the arrival of Lord Hari and not seeing (any chances) of the return of the deputed Brāhmaṇa, began to think as follows:

devanagari: अहो त्रियामान्तरित उद्वाहो मेऽल्पराधसः । नागच्छत्यरविन्दाक्षो नाहं वेद्म्यत्र कारणम् । सोऽपि नावर्ततेऽद्यापि मत्सन्देशहरो द्विजः ॥ २३ ...
english_devnagari: aho triyāmāntarita udvāho me nāgacchatyaravindākṣo nāhaṃ vedmyatra kāraṇam so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: “Alas! Only one night has remained for the celebration of the marriage. I am afraid I am unfortunate with little merit (to my credit). The Lotus-eyed Lord has not come as yet, nor do I know its reason. The Brāhmaṇa who was my emissary with that message, has also not returned as yet.

devanagari: अपि मय्यनवद्यात्मा दृष्ट्वा किञ्चिज्जुगुप्सितम् । मत्पाणिग्रहणे नूनं नायाति हि कृतोद्यमः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: api mayyanavadyātmā dṛṣṭvā kiñcijjugupsitam matpāṇigrahaṇe nūnaṃ nāyāti hi kṛtodyamaḥ

translation: Is it that the noble-souled Lord who made preparations for coming here has seen something censurable in me and hence does not come for the acceptance of my hand?

devanagari: दुर्भगाया न मे धाता नानुकूलो महेश्वरः । देवी वा विमुखी गौरी रुद्राणी गिरिजा सती ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: durbhagāyā na me dhātā nānukūlo maheśvaraḥ devī vā vimukhī gaurī rudrāṇī girijā satī
translation: To me who am unfortunate, neither god Brahmā nor god Śiva is favourable and the fair goddess Pārvatī the spouse of god Śiva, that chaste daughter of Himālayas too seems to be averse to me.”

devanagari: एवं चिन्तयती बाला गोविन्दहृतमानसा । न्यमीलयत कालज्ञा नेत्रे चाश्रुकलाकुले ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ cintayatī bālā govindahṛtamānasā nyamīlayata kālajñā netre cāśrukalākule

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: While the girl whose mind was thus fixed on Lord Kṛṣṇa (as if carried away by him) was pondering thus, her eyes were bedimmed with tears. But as she knew what was proper for that particular time (viz. not shedding tears), she closed her eyes.

devanagari: एवं वध्वाः प्रतीक्षन्त्या गोविन्दागमनं नृप । वाम ऊरुर्भुजो नेत्रमस्फुरन्प्रियभाषिणः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vadhvāḥ pratīkṣantyā govindāgamanaṃ nṛpa vāma ūrurbhujo netramasphuranpriyabhāṣiṇaḥ
translation: In this way, of the bride who was eagerly waiting for the coming of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the left thigh, arm, and eye began to throb indicating thereby auspicious and happy news.

devanagari: अथ कृष्णविनिर्दिष्टः स एव द्विजसत्तमः । अन्तःपुरचरीं देवीं राजपुत्रीं ददर्श ह ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: atha kṛṣṇavinirdiṣṭaḥ sa eva dvijasattamaḥ antaḥpuracarīṃ devīṃ rājaputrīṃ dadarśa ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: That very excellent Brāhmaṇa who was directed by Kṛṣṇa (after his arrival in the park near Kuṇḍina) saw the glorious princess in the harem.

devanagari: सा तं प्रहृष्टवदनमव्यग्रात्मगतिं सती । आलक्ष्य लक्षणाभिज्ञा समपृच्छच्छुचिस्मिता ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sā taṃ prahṛṣṭavadanamavyagrātmagatiṃ satī ālakṣya lakṣaṇābhijñā samapṛcchacchucismitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ praś class 6 verb]

translation: Seeing his cheerful countenance and gait with confident steps (indicating a success of his mission) the virtuous girl who was expert in guessing the indications, asked him with a broad smile.

devanagari: तस्या आवेदयत्प्राप्तं शशंस यदुनन्दनम् । उक्तं च सत्यवचनमात्मोपनयनं प्रति ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tasyā āvedayatprāptaṃ śaśaṃsa yadunandanam uktaṃ ca satyavacanamātmopanayanaṃ prati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: He informed her that Kṛṣṇa, the delight of Yadus had arrived and assured her of his definite promise of taking her away (supra verse 3) and that he was brought in his chariot by Kṛṣṇa Himself.

devanagari: तमागतं समाज्ञाय वैदर्भी हृष्टमानसा । न पश्यन्ती ब्राह्मणाय प्रियमन्यन्ननाम सा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāgataṃ samājñāya vaidarbhī hṛṣṭamānasā na paśyantī brāhmaṇāya priyamanyannanāma sā
translation:
Knowing definitely that he (Kṛṣṇa) had arrived (at Kuṇḍinapura), the princess of Vidarbha was over-joyed at heart. (In her exultation) she could not decide what gift or boon be conferred on the Brāhmaṇa for his highly valuable service. And she simply bowed down to him[2] (and later paid him sumptuously).
.earning that both Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa had arrived out of curiosity to witness the celebration of his daughter’s marriage, king Bhīṣmaka went forth to receive them with a flourish of trumpets, taking with him proper articles of worship and presents.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Or: Rukmiṇī was an incarnation of Lakṣmī, the goddess of wealth. All people who pay respects to her are bestowed with all kinds of wealth. She decided, “When I, Lakṣmī, pay respects to this Brāhmaṇa, he will automatically become more affluent than all others.”

devanagari: प्राप्तौ श्रुत्वा स्वदुहितुरुद्वाहप्रेक्षणोत्सुकौ । अभ्ययात्तूर्यघोषेण रामकृष्णौ समर्हणैः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: prāptau śrutvā svaduhiturudvāhaprekṣaṇotsukau abhyayāttūryaghoṣeṇa rāmakṛṣṇau samarhaṇaiḥ

devanagari: मधुपर्कमुपानीय वासांसि विरजांसि सः । उपायनान्यभीष्टानि विधिवत्समपूजयत् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: madhuparkamupānīya vāsāṃsi virajāṃsi saḥ upāyanānyabhīṣṭāni vidhivatsamapūjayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pūj class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: (Being highly intelligent, he guessed that Kṛṣṇa had arrived for marrying his daughter for which he was quite willing). So he worshipped Kṛṣṇa with due formalities suitable after the arrival of the bridegroom and he brought Madhuparka (a preparation of honey and curds—a customary offer to a bridegroom after arrival on the eve of the marriage ceremony), excellent (lit. well-washed) garments and other desirable presents (of various kinds) and worshipped them as enjoined in scriptures.

devanagari: तयोर्निवेशनं श्रीमदुपाकल्प्य महामतिः । ससैन्ययोः सानुगयोरातिथ्यं विदधे यथा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorniveśanaṃ śrīmadupākalpya mahāmatiḥ sasainyayoḥ sānugayorātithyaṃ vidadhe yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ kḷp class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kḷp ]
[vocative single from √ kḷp class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kḷp ]

translation:
The high-minded monarch arranged for a spacious, luxurious accommodation for them.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
34(A). It was quite spacious and suitable for them but in a garden on the outskirts of Kuṇḍinapura, as the city was overcrowded with kings like Jarāsandha and others.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Probably to avoid an outbreak of conflict if the two inimical camps are accommodated in the town.

devanagari: एवं राज्ञां समेतानां यथावीर्यं यथावयः । यथाबलं यथावित्तं सर्वैः कामैः समर्हयत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ rājñāṃ sametānāṃ yathāvīryaṃ yathāvayaḥ yathābalaṃ yathāvittaṃ sarvaiḥ kāmaiḥ samarhayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ arh ], [vocative single from √ arh ], [accusative single from √ arh ]

translation: He also arranged for the hospitable reception of Kṛṣṇa’s followers and army, in a proper manner.

devanagari: कृष्णमागतमाकर्ण्य विदर्भपुरवासिनः । आगत्य नेत्राञ्जलिभिः पपुस्तन्मुखपङ्कजम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇamāgatamākarṇya vidarbhapuravāsinaḥ āgatya netrāñjalibhiḥ papustanmukhapaṅkajam
translation: In this way, he received all other kings who arrived there, each according to his prowess, age, military power and affluence, catering to them all that they desired.

devanagari: अस्यैव भार्या भवितुं रुक्मिण्यर्हति नापरा । असावप्यनवद्यात्मा भैष्म्याः समुचितः पतिः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: asyaiva bhāryā bhavituṃ rukmiṇyarhati nāparā asāvapyanavadyātmā bhaiṣmyāḥ samucitaḥ patiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative passive first single]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ bhṛ ]

translation: Hearing that Kṛṣṇa had arrived, the citizens of Kuṇḍinapura came to see him and (as it were) drank (to their heart’s content the honey of beauty) of his lotus-face with both their eyes as if they were the joined palms for drinking).

devanagari: किञ्चित्सुचरितं यन्नस्तेन तुष्टस्त्रिलोककृत् । अनुगृह्णातु गृह्णातु वैदर्भ्याः पाणिमच्युतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kiñcitsucaritaṃ yannastena tuṣṭastrilokakṛt anugṛhṇātu gṛhṇātu vaidarbhyāḥ pāṇimacyutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: (They spoke amongst themselves): ‘Only Rukmiṇī and no other girl deserves to be his consort, and he alone of faultlessly beautiful personality is the suitable consort for Rukmiṇī, the daughter of Bhīṣma.

devanagari: एवं प्रेमकलाबद्धा वदन्ति स्म पुरौकसः । कन्या चान्तःपुरात्प्रागाद्भटैर्गुप्ताम्बिकालयम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ premakalābaddhā vadanti sma puraukasaḥ kanyā cāntaḥpurātprāgādbhaṭairguptāmbikālayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: May the Lord, the creator of the three worlds, be pleased with whatever little merit we possess and as his grace (showered on us) may the imperishable Lord Kṛṣṇa accept the hand of the princess of Vidarbha.”

devanagari: पद्भ्यां विनिर्ययौ द्रष्टुं भवान्याः पादपल्लवम् । सा चानुध्यायती सम्यङ्मुकुन्दचरणाम्बुजम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: padbhyāṃ viniryayau draṣṭuṃ bhavānyāḥ pādapallavam sā cānudhyāyatī samyaṅmukundacaraṇāmbujam
translation: While the citizens who were attached to Rukmiṇī by bonds of affection, were speaking thus with one another, the bride, guarded by soldiers, started from theharem to the shrine of Ambikā (goddess Pārvatī).

devanagari: यतवाङ्मातृभिः सार्धं सखीभिः परिवारिता । गुप्ता राजभटैः शूरैः सन्नद्धैरुद्यतायुधैः । मृडङ्गशङ्खपणवास्तूर्यभेर्यश्च जघ्निरे ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yatavāṅmātṛbhiḥ sārdhaṃ sakhībhiḥ parivāritā guptā rājabhaṭaiḥ śūraiḥ sannaddhairudyatāyudhaiḥ mṛḍaṅgaśaṅkhapaṇavāstūryabheryaśca jaghnire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ gup class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ gup class 4 verb]

translation:
Deeply contemplating on the lotus-feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, she went on foot to see and worship the foliage-like tender feet of goddess Bhavānī.
[Padaratnāvalī’s. Text adds:]
40(A). Kṛṣṇa, the delight of Yādavas went there in the company of some selected followers.

devanagari: नानोपहार बलिभिर्वारमुख्याः सहस्रशः । स्रग्गन्धवस्त्राभरणैर्द्विजपत्न्यः स्वलङ्कृताः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: nānopahāra balibhirvāramukhyāḥ sahasraśaḥ sraggandhavastrābharaṇairdvijapatnyaḥ svalaṅkṛtāḥ
translation: She was accompanied by thousands of prominent courtesans carrying with them a variety of offerings and articles of worship and by Brāhmaṇa women (whose husbands were alive) tastefully adorned with garlands of flowers, perfumes (or sandal paste) excellent garments and ornaments.

devanagari: गायन्त्यश्च स्तुवन्तश्च गायका वाद्यवादकाः । परिवार्य वधूं जग्मुः सूतमागधवन्दिनः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: gāyantyaśca stuvantaśca gāyakā vādyavādakāḥ parivārya vadhūṃ jagmuḥ sūtamāgadhavandinaḥ

translation: Songsters singing songs and musicians playing on their instruments as well as bards and panegyrists like Sūta, Māgadha and Bandins surrounded the bride glorifying her in songs.

devanagari: आसाद्य देवीसदनं धौतपादकराम्बुजा । उपस्पृश्य शुचिः शान्ता प्रविवेशाम्बिकान्तिकम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: āsādya devīsadanaṃ dhautapādakarāmbujā upaspṛśya śuciḥ śāntā praviveśāmbikāntikam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Arriving at the shrine of the goddess[4], she washed her lotus-like hands and feet. Purifying herself with sipping the water thrice as ācamana, she entered with a serene mind in the inner apartment—into the presence of the goddess Pārvatī.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā’s v.l.—she entered the shrine of the goddess.

devanagari: तां वै प्रवयसो बालां विधिज्ञा विप्रयोषितः । भवानीं वन्दयांचक्रुर्भवपत्नीं भवान्विताम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ vai pravayaso bālāṃ vidhijñā viprayoṣitaḥ bhavānīṃ vandayāṃcakrurbhavapatnīṃ bhavānvitām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Aged Brāhmaṇa ladies who were well-versed in the (pre-marital) rituals, guided the bride in the worship and paying respects to the goddess Pārvatī, the Consort of Śiva, along with god Śiva as well.

devanagari: नमस्ये त्वाम्बिकेऽभीक्ष्णं स्वसन्तानयुतां शिवाम् । भूयात्पतिर्मे भगवान्कृष्णस्तदनुमोदताम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: namasye tvāmbike bhūyātpatirme bhagavānkṛṣṇastadanumodatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: (Rukmiṇī prayed:) I constantly pay my respects to you Oh auspicious[5] Mother goddess, along with your children (Gaṇeśa and Kārttikeya). May Lord Kṛṣṇa become my consort. May this prayer of mine be granted.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Śivā—implies respects to Lord Śiva with whom she (the goddess) shares the name and person.

devanagari: अद्भिर्गन्धाक्षतैर्धूपैर्वासःस्रङ्माल्य भूषणैः । नानोपहारबलिभिः प्रदीपावलिभिः पृथक् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: adbhirgandhākṣatairdhūpairvāsaḥsraṅmālya bhūṣaṇaiḥ nānopahārabalibhiḥ pradīpāvalibhiḥ pṛthak
translation: She worshipped the goddess Pārvatī in several ways by sprinkling her with water, applying sandal-paste and unbroken rice-grains, varieties of incense, with (a rich variety of) clothes, garlands of flowers, ornaments, various kinds of articles of worship and offerings (variety of edibles) and with rows of lights (waving them round her).

devanagari: विप्रस्त्रियः पतिमतीस्तथा तैः समपूजयत् । लवणापूपताम्बूल कण्ठसूत्रफलेक्षुभिः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: viprastriyaḥ patimatīstathā taiḥ samapūjayat lavaṇāpūpatāmbūla kaṇṭhasūtraphalekṣubhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pūj class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: With similar articles she worshipped Brāhmaṇa women Suvāsinīs—(whose husbands are alive) in the same way, offering them salt, apūpa (fried cakes), betel leaves, maṅgalasūtra. (the sacred thread to be worn round the neck by Suvāsinīs), fruits (of various kinds) and (pieces of) sugarcane.

devanagari: तस्यै स्त्रियस्ताः प्रददुः शेषां युयुजुराशिषः । ताभ्यो देव्यै नमश्चक्रे शेषां च जगृहे वधूः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyai striyastāḥ pradaduḥ śeṣāṃ yuyujurāśiṣaḥ tābhyo devyai namaścakre śeṣāṃ ca jagṛhe vadhūḥ

translation: The Brāhmaṇa ladies gave her some part (of what she offered as worship to the goddess Ambikā) and gave their blessings (in addition) along with them. Rukmiṇī bowed down to them and and to the queen-mother and accepted the things offered to her (as prasāda).

devanagari: मुनिव्रतमथ त्यक्त्वा निश्चक्रामाम्बिकागृहात् । प्रगृह्य पाणिना भृत्यां रत्नमुद्रोपशोभिना ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: munivratamatha tyaktvā niścakrāmāmbikāgṛhāt pragṛhya pāṇinā bhṛtyāṃ ratnamudropaśobhinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: 2 Rukmiṇī entered (the inner part of the shrine) in the presence of Śacī, the beloved of the lord of gods. She bowed to the feet of Śacī whose lotus-feet were not worshipped by her for a pretty long time. Then she issued out of that temple with many umbrellas being held over her and fanned by waving of a number of chowries.

devanagari: तां देवमायामिव धीरमोहिनीं सुमध्यमां कुण्डलमण्डिताननाम् । श्यामां नितम्बार्पितरत्नमेखलां व्यञ्जत्स्तनीं कुन्तलशङ्कितेक्षणाम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ devamāyāmiva dhīramohinīṃ sumadhyamāṃ kuṇḍalamaṇḍitānanām śyāmāṃ nitambārpitaratnamekhalāṃ vyañjatstanīṃ kuntalaśaṅkitekṣaṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [locative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb]

[nominative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [locative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb]

[nominative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śaṅk class 1 verb]

translation: The glorious warriors who assembled there found themselves tormented with the passion of love and became perplexed at seeing her, who like the deluding potency (Māyā) of the Lord, was enchanting to the warriors, with her beautiful slender waist, and countenance beautified with the earrings; she was sixteen years of age[6], wore a girdle of precious stones around her waist: had protruding breasts (showing attainment of womanhood); her eyes were unsteady, appearing as if afraid of her over-hanging locks of hair. Beaming with bright smile, her white jasmine-bud like teeth appeared reddish as they were tinged with the splendour of her red lips-reḍ like the Bimba fruit. Her gait was like that of a royal swan and her feet were radiant with the splendour of jingling anklets.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Śyāmā—who had not attained puberty—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: शुचिस्मितां बिम्बफलाधरद्युति शोणायमानद्विजकुन्दकुड्मलाम् । पदा चलन्तीं कलहंसगामिनीं सिञ्जत्कलानूपुरधामशोभिना ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: śucismitāṃ bimbaphalādharadyuti śoṇāyamānadvijakundakuḍmalām padā calantīṃ kalahaṃsagāminīṃ siñjatkalānūpuradhāmaśobhinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mān class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mān class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ mān class 1 verb]

translation: The glorious warriors who assembled there found themselves tormented with the passion of love and became perplexed at seeing her, who like the deluding potency (Māyā) of the Lord, was enchanting to the warriors, with her beautiful slender waist, and countenance beautified with the earrings; she was sixteen years of age[6], wore a girdle of precious stones around her waist: had protruding breasts (showing attainment of womanhood); her eyes were unsteady, appearing as if afraid of her over-hanging locks of hair. Beaming with bright smile, her white jasmine-bud like teeth appeared reddish as they were tinged with the splendour of her red lips-reḍ like the Bimba fruit. Her gait was like that of a royal swan and her feet were radiant with the splendour of jingling anklets.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Śyāmā—who had not attained puberty—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: अथ त्रिपञ्चाशत्तमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 53 ॥
english_devnagari: atha tripañcāśattamo
translation: The glorious warriors who assembled there found themselves tormented with the passion of love and became perplexed at seeing her, who like the deluding potency (Māyā) of the Lord, was enchanting to the warriors, with her beautiful slender waist, and countenance beautified with the earrings; she was sixteen years of age[6], wore a girdle of precious stones around her waist: had protruding breasts (showing attainment of womanhood); her eyes were unsteady, appearing as if afraid of her over-hanging locks of hair. Beaming with bright smile, her white jasmine-bud like teeth appeared reddish as they were tinged with the splendour of her red lips-reḍ like the Bimba fruit. Her gait was like that of a royal swan and her feet were radiant with the splendour of jingling anklets.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Śyāmā—who had not attained puberty—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: पेतुः क्षितौ गजरथाश्वगता विमूढा यात्राच्छलेन हरयेऽर्पयतीं स्वशोभाम् । सैवं शनैश्चलयती चलपद्मकोशौ प्राप्तिं तदा भगवतः प्रसमीक्षमाणा ॥ ५...
english_devnagari: petuḥ kṣitau gajarathāśvagatā vimūḍhā yātrācchalena haraye saivaṃ śanaiścalayatī calapadmakośau prāptiṃ tadā bhagavataḥ prasamīkṣamāṇā

translation: Hypnotized on seeing Rukmiṇī who under the pretex of the religious pilgrimage (to the shrine of the goddess), had come to offer her beauty to Lord Hari, the kings whose hearts were captivated by her sublime smile and bashful looks, dropped down their weapons (without being aware of it), and fell senseless on the ground even though they were mounted on elephants, chariots and horses.

devanagari: उत्सार्य वामकरजैरलकानपङ्गैः प्राप्तान्ह्रियैक्षत नृपान्ददृशेऽच्युतं च । तां राजकन्यां रथमारुरक्षतीं जहार कृष्णो द्विषतां समीक्षताम् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: utsārya vāmakarajairalakānapaṅgaiḥ prāptānhriyaikṣata nṛpāndadṛśe tāṃ rājakanyāṃ rathamārurakṣatīṃ jahāra kṛṣṇo dviṣatāṃ samīkṣatām

translation: At that time, she was eagerly awaiting the arrival of the Lord and hence was slowly moving (her feet resembling) a pair of lotus-buds. Removing aside her hair flowing over her eyes with her fingers, she cast bashful side-glances to have an over-view of the kings that assembled there. And Lo! she noticed him there.

devanagari: रथं समारोप्य सुपर्णलक्षणं राजन्यचक्रं परिभूय माधवः । ततो ययौ रामपुरोगमः शनैः शृगालमध्यादिव भागहृद्धरिः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: rathaṃ samāropya suparṇalakṣaṇaṃ rājanyacakraṃ paribhūya mādhavaḥ tato yayau rāmapurogamaḥ śanaiḥ śṛgālamadhyādiva bhāgahṛddhariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Even while the enemies were looking on, Kṛṣṇa snatched away the princess who was desirous of ascending his chariot; he seated her in his chariot with the ensign of Garuḍa (emblem of Garuda on the flag) setting at nought the whole lot of Kṣattriyas assembled there. Kṛṣṇa, the Consort of Lakṣmī, then slowly (and fearlessly) departed from the place (to Dvārakā) with his forces headed by Balarāma, like a lion seizing his own share of prey from the midst of a pack of jackals.

devanagari: तं मानिनः स्वाभिभवं यशःक्षयं । परे जरासन्धमुखा न सेहिरे । अहो धिगस्मान्यश आत्तधन्वनां । गोपैर्हृतं केशरिणां मृगैरिव ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ māninaḥ svābhibhavaṃ yaśaḥkṣayaṃ pare jarāsandhamukhā na sehire aho dhigasmānyaśa āttadhanvanāṃ gopairhṛtaṃ keśariṇāṃ mṛgairiva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation:
[7] Other proud kings under the leadership of Jarāsandha did not tolerate this discomfiture and loss of reputation (by Kṛṣṇa’s carrying away Rukmiṇī in the teeth of their opposition). They exclaimed: “Fie upon us who, although armed with bows, have been deprived of our reputation and glory by cowherds just as the lions are deprived of their kingly position by the deer.”
[back to top]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
This is the 1st verse of the next chapter in Padaratnāvalī
